| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_norm_le_balanced_bound` | **symm-energy norm upper bound**: `‖bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N‖ ≤ \|Λ\|²·N²/8 + \|Λ\|·N/2`. Both terms bounded via AM-GM (`\|A\|·\|¬A\| ≤ (\|Λ\|/2)²`) and `min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|) ≤ \|Λ\|/2` (PR #2875, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmNormLeBound.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_via_imbalance_sq` | **bridge identity** between the symm-energy real part and the imbalance-weight squared real part: `8·((bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re + min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N) + \|Λ\|²·N² = 4·((bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N).re)²`. Algebraic consequence of `4·\|A\|·\|¬A\| = (\|A\|+\|¬A\|)² - (\|A\|-\|¬A\|)²` combined with `\|A\| + \|¬A\| = \|Λ\|` (PR #2870) (PR #2876, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmViaImbalance.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_mul_self_eq_re_mul_self` / `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_via_imbalance_norm_sq` | **norm-squared = real-part squared** (`‖biw‖·‖biw‖ = biw.re·biw.re`, since `biw` is real, PR #2825) and the resulting **bridge identity in `‖biw‖²` form**: `8·((bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re + min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N) + \|Λ\|²·N² = 4·‖bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‖²`. Substitutes `(biw.re)² = ‖biw‖²` into PR #2876; natural form for Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3 where the predicted spin magnitude `‖biw‖ = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N/2` appears directly (PR #2877, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReViaImbalanceNormSq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_eq_half_imbalance_norm_sq_sub` | **explicit closed form** of the symm-energy real part: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re = ‖bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‖²/2 − \|Λ\|²·N²/8 − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`. Direct `linarith` rearrangement of PR #2877's bridge identity. Expresses the predicted symmetric ground-state energy as a function of the spin magnitude squared, the lattice cardinality, and the minor sublattice count (PR #2878, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReEqHalfImbalanceNormSq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_ge_neg_balanced_bound` | **lower bound on the symm-energy real part**: `−\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N ≤ (bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re`. Direct from PR #2878 by dropping the non-negative `‖biw‖²/2` term. Combined with PR #2844 `(symm).re ≤ 0`, gives the sandwich `−\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − min·N ≤ symm.re ≤ 0`; equality at the lower bound iff `‖biw‖ = 0` (for `N ≥ 1` this characterises balanced sublattices) (PR #2880, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReGeNegBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_balanced_re_eq_cardinality` | **balanced-case symm-energy real part in `\|Λ\|` form**: when `\|A\| = \|¬A\|`, `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re = −\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − \|Λ\|·N/2`. Combines PR #2842's balanced complex form (`= −\|A\|²·N²/2 − \|A\|·N`) with PR #2870's cardinality identity `2·\|A\| = \|Λ\|`. Saturates the PR #2880 lower bound at balanced sublattices (PR #2881, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmBalancedReEqCardinality.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_ge_global_min_bound` | **configuration-independent global lower bound on the symm-energy real part**: `−\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − \|Λ\|·N/2 ≤ (bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re` for any `A`. Combines PR #2880 (`A`-dependent lower bound `−\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`) with `min ≤ \|Λ\|/2` (from PR #2870). Saturated at balanced sublattices (PR #2881): balanced configurations realise the global minimum of the symm-predicted energy real part (PR #2882, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReGeGlobalMin.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_le_neg_min` | **refined upper bound on the symm-energy real part**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re ≤ −min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`. Direct from PR #2878 closed form + PR #2874 imbalance-norm `m_max` upper bound (squared). Refines PR #2844 (`(symm).re ≤ 0`) when `min > 0`; combined with PR #2880 gives the tight `min`-aware sandwich `−\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − min·N ≤ symm.re ≤ −min·N` (PR #2883, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReLeNegMin.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_sub_predictedSymm` | **Néel-to-predicted-min energy gap**: `<Φ_Néel(A, N) \| Ĥ_toy_S \| Φ_Néel(A, N)> − bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N = min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`. The Néel state's energy expectation is above the symmetric predicted minimum by exactly `min·N`; gap vanishes at saturated edges and is maximised at balanced sublattices. Consistent with Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3: Néel is a candidate but the true GS energy is lower by the sublattice-Casimir diagonal contribution (PR #2884, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSPredictedSymmGap.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_lt_neg_min_of_nondegenerate` | **strict refined upper bound on the symm-energy real part** (non-degenerate): `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re < −min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N` when `\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`. Strict version of PR #2883, immediate from PR #2843 (`_re_eq = −\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2 − min·N`) since `\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N² > 0` (PR #2885, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReLtNegMinStrict.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_via_imbalance_norm_sq` | **Néel-state expectation real part in `‖biw‖²` form**: `(<Φ_Néel\|Ĥ_toy_S\|Φ_Néel>).re = ‖bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‖²/2 − \|Λ\|²·N²/8`. Combines PR #2884 (Néel–predicted-min gap `= min·N`) with PR #2878 (predicted-symm closed form): the `min·N` terms cancel, leaving a clean form depending only on the predicted spin magnitude squared and the lattice cardinality (PR #2886, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationReViaImbalanceNormSq.lean`) |
| `min_cardA_cardNotA_mul_N_eq_half_card_times_N_sub_imbalance_norm` | **linear identity bridging `min·N` and `‖biw‖`**: `min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N = \|Λ\|·N/2 − ‖bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‖`. Direct from PR #2826 (`‖biw‖ = \|\|A\|−\|¬A\|\|·N/2`) + PR #2870 (`\|A\|+\|¬A\| = \|Λ\|`) via the elementary identity `2·min = (\|A\|+\|¬A\|) − \|\|A\|−\|¬A\|\|`. Enables `min·N`-free reformulations of all Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3 (γ-4) energy bounds (PR #2887, file `Quantum/SpinS/MinNEqHalfCardTimesNMinusImbalanceNorm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_via_imbalance_norm_only` | **univariate `‖biw‖`-parameterisation of the symm-energy real part**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re = ‖biw‖²/2 + ‖biw‖ − \|Λ\|²·N²/8 − \|Λ\|·N/2`. Direct from PR #2878 (closed form with `min·N`) + PR #2887 (`min·N` identity): eliminates `min`-dependence, parameterising the predicted GS energy as a univariate quadratic in the predicted spin magnitude (PR #2888, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReViaImbalanceNormOnly.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_lt_mMax_of_nondegenerate` | **strict `m_max` upper bound on imbalance norm** (non-degenerate): `‖bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‖ < \|Λ\|·N/2` when `\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`. Strict version of PR #2874; via PR #2887 identity `min·N = \|Λ\|·N/2 − ‖biw‖`, strictness reduces to `min·N > 0` at non-degeneracy. Saturated at `min = 0` (saturated edges) (PR #2889, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormLtMMaxNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_mono_in_imbalance_norm` | **monotonicity of `symm.re` in `‖biw‖`**: for `A₁, A₂ : Λ → Bool`, `‖biw(A₁, N)‖ ≤ ‖biw(A₂, N)‖ ⟹ (symm(A₁, N)).re ≤ (symm(A₂, N)).re`. Direct from PR #2888 univariate form + monotonicity of `x ↦ x²/2 + x` on `[0, ∞)` (derivative `x + 1 > 0`). Characterises balanced as global energy minimiser and saturated as maximiser (PR #2890, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReMonoInImbalanceNorm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_strict_mono_in_imbalance_norm` | **strict monotonicity of `symm.re` in `‖biw‖`**: `‖biw(A₁, N)‖ < ‖biw(A₂, N)‖ ⟹ (symm(A₁, N)).re < (symm(A₂, N)).re`. Strict version of PR #2890 (PR #2891, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReStrictMonoInImbalanceNorm.lean`) |
| `cardA_times_cardNotA_times_N_sq_eq_quarter_card_sq_times_N_sq_sub_imbalance_norm_sq` | **AFM cross-term bridge**: `\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N² = \|Λ\|²·N²/4 − ‖bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N‖²`. Direct from PR #2876 (`8·(symm.re + min·N) + \|Λ\|²·N² = 4·(biw.re)²`) + PR #2877 (`‖biw‖² = (biw.re)²`) + PR #2843 (closed form). Expresses the Néel expectation magnitude in terms of imbalance norm and lattice cardinality (PR #2892, file `Quantum/SpinS/CardATimesCardNotATimesNSqViaImbalanceNormSq.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_sub_predictedSymm_re_pos_of_nondegenerate` | **strict positive Néel-vs-predicted-min energy gap** (non-degenerate): `0 < (<Φ_Néel\|Ĥ_toy_S\|Φ_Néel> − bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re` when `\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`. Strict version of PR #2884; since the gap equals `min·N` and `min, N > 0` (PR #2893, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSPredictedSymmGapPosNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_eq_zero_iff_card_zero` | **real-part iff form** at `N ≥ 1`: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re = 0 ↔ \|A\| = 0 ∨ \|¬A\| = 0`. Real-part mirror of PR #2864 (complex iff). Forward: contrapositive of PR #2845 strict negativity; reverse: PR #2849/2850 saturated-edge zero (PR #2894, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReEqZeroIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_gt_global_min_bound_of_unbalanced` | **strict global lower bound at unbalanced**: `−\|Λ\|²·N²/8 − \|Λ\|·N/2 < (bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re` when `‖biw‖ > 0`. Direct from PR #2888 univariate form: `‖biw‖²/2 + ‖biw‖ > 0` at `‖biw‖ > 0`. Strict version of PR #2882 (PR #2895, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymmReGtGlobalMinOfUnbalanced.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_re_via_imbalance_norm_sq` | **Néel-state `(Ŝ_tot)²` expectation real part in `‖biw‖²` form**: `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot)²\|Φ_Néel>).re = ‖biw‖² + \|Λ\|·N/2`. Direct from γ-4 step 225 (`= ((\|A\|-\|¬A\|)·N/2)² + \|Λ\|·N/2`) + PR #2877 helper (`‖biw‖² = (biw.re)²`). Connects the Néel Casimir expectation to the predicted spin magnitude squared (`‖biw‖² = (predicted S_tot)²`) plus the lattice-size offset `m_max = \|Λ\|·N/2` (PR #2896, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSSquaredReViaImbalanceNormSq.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_re_balanced` | **balanced Néel `(Ŝ_tot)²` expectation = `\|Λ\|·N/2`**: at `\|A\| = \|¬A\|`, `N ≥ 1`, `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot)²\|Φ_Néel>).re = \|Λ\|·N/2`. Direct from PR #2896 + `‖biw‖ = 0` at balanced (via PR #2854). Minimum value of the `(Ŝ_tot)²` expectation across all bipartitions (PR #2897, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSSquaredBalanced.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_re_saturated_cardNotA_zero` / `_cardA_zero` | **saturated Néel `(Ŝ_tot)²` expectation = `m_max·(m_max+1)`**: at `\|¬A\| = 0` (or `\|A\| = 0`), `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot)²\|Φ_Néel>).re = (\|Λ\|·N/2)·(\|Λ\|·N/2 + 1)`. Direct from PR #2896 + `‖biw‖ = \|Λ\|·N/2` at saturated (PR #2851/2852). Néel state at saturated edges IS a `(Ŝ_tot)²` eigenvector at the fully-polarised ferromagnetic maximum-spin eigenvalue (PR #2898, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSSquaredSaturated.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_sub_predictedSymm_re_ge_N_of_nondegenerate` | **quantitative Néel-vs-predictedSymm gap lower bound**: at `\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `(<Φ_Néel\|Ĥ_toy_S\|Φ_Néel> − bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re ≥ N`. Refines PR #2893's strict positivity since `min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|) ≥ 1` at non-degenerate, gap = `min·N ≥ N` (PR #2899, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSPredictedSymmGapGeN.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_re_sub_predicted_eq_min_N` | **Néel-vs-predicted (Ŝ_tot)² gap = min·N**: `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot)²\|Φ_Néel>).re − (‖biw‖² + ‖biw‖) = min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`. Direct parallel of energy gap PR #2884 to the (Ŝ_tot)² Casimir: the Néel state's `(Ŝ_tot)²` expectation is exactly `min·N` above the predicted `S_tot·(S_tot+1) = ‖biw‖·(‖biw‖+1)` eigenvalue (Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3). | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_re_sub_predicted_ge_N_of_nondegenerate` | **quantitative Néel-vs-predicted (Ŝ_tot)² gap lower bound**: at `\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot)²\|Φ_Néel>).re − (‖biw‖² + ‖biw‖) ≥ N`. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_expectation_re_via_imbalance_norm_sq` | **Néel-state `(Ŝ_tot^(3))²` expectation real part in `‖biw‖²` form**: `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot^(3))²\|Φ_Néel>).re = ‖biw‖²`. | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_expectation_re_eq_imbalance_re` | **Néel-state linear `Ŝ_tot^(3)` expectation real part = `biw.re`**: `(<Φ_Néel\|Ŝ_tot^(3)\|Φ_Néel>).re = (bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N).re`. Both equal `(\|A\|-\|¬A\|)·N/2`. Signed analog of `‖biw‖` (PR #2904 squared form) (PR #2905, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSOp3ReEqBiwRe.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_re_eq_half_card_times_N` | **Transverse Casimir Néel expectation real part = `\|Λ\|·N/2`** (bipartition-independent): `(<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_tot^(1))² + (Ŝ_tot^(2))²\|Φ_Néel>).re = \|Λ\|·N/2`. Together with PRs #2896, #2904 gives the operator-level Casimir decomposition on Néel: the bipartition dependence of `<(Ŝ_tot)²>_Néel` is fully concentrated in the z-axis `(Ŝ_tot^(3))²` component (PR #2906, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSOp12SqReEqHalfCardTimesN.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1_expectation_re_eq_zero` / `_Op2_` | **Linear transverse Néel expectations vanish**: `(<Φ_Néel\|Ŝ_tot^(1)\|Φ_Néel>).re = 0` and `(<Φ_Néel\|Ŝ_tot^(2)\|Φ_Néel>).re = 0`. Direct from γ-4 step 241 (complex form `= 0`) + `Complex.zero_re`. Completes the linear part of the Néel operator-level decomposition: z-axis = `biw.re`, transverse = `0` (PR #2907, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSOp1Op2ReEqZero.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_zero_variance` | **Zero `Ŝ_tot^(3)` variance on Néel**: `<(Ŝ_tot^(3))²>_Néel.re = (<Ŝ_tot^(3)>_Néel.re)²`. | `neelStateOfS_sublatticeSpinSquaredS_expectation` / `_complement_` | **Néel sublattice Casimir expectations**: `<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_A)²\|Φ_Néel> = s_A·(s_A+1)` and `<Φ_Néel\|(Ŝ_¬A)²\|Φ_Néel> = s_B·(s_B+1)` where `s_A = \|A\|·N/2`, `s_B = \|¬A\|·N/2`. Direct from existing eigenvector relations + `neelStateOfS_inner_self`. | `neelStateOfS_mem_joint_sublattice_casimir_eigenspace` | **Néel ∈ joint sublattice-Casimir eigenspace**: `Φ_Néel(A, N) ∈ eigenspace((Ŝ_A)², s_A·(s_A+1)) ⊓ eigenspace((Ŝ_¬A)², s_B·(s_B+1))`. Two-thirds of the predicted GS subspace conditions. Néel is NOT in the full predicted GS subspace because its `(Ŝ_tot)²` expectation (`‖biw‖²+\|Λ\|·N/2`, PR #2896) exceeds the predicted eigenvalue `‖biw‖·(‖biw‖+1)` by `min·N` (PR #2901) (PR #2913, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSMemJointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace.lean`) |
| `neelConfigOfS_eq_const_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `neelStateOfS_eq_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `neelStateOfS_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **Néel = all-aligned + GS membership at saturated `\|¬A\| = 0`**: at saturated, `neelConfigOfS A N = fun _ => 0`, hence `Φ_Néel(A, N) = allAlignedStateS Λ N 0`. Combined with PR #2782's all-up GS witness, gives `Φ_Néel(A, N) ∈ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` at `\|¬A\| = 0`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_balanced` | **Balanced-case Néel toy-Hamiltonian expectation = −\|Λ\|²·N²/8**: at `\|A\| = \|¬A\|`, `N ≥ 1`, `(<Φ_Néel\|Ĥ_toy_S\|Φ_Néel>).re = −\|Λ\|²·N²/8`. Direct from PR #2886 (`‖biw‖²/2 − \|Λ\|²·N²/8`) + `‖biw‖ = 0` at balanced. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `_cardA_zero` | **Saturated-edge Néel toy-Hamiltonian expectation = 0**: at `\|¬A\| = 0` (or `\|A\| = 0`), `(<Φ_Néel\|Ĥ_toy_S\|Φ_Néel>).re = 0`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_eq_joint_eigenspace_of_cardNotA_zero` | **Predicted GS = joint Casimir eigenspace at saturated `\|¬A\| = 0`**: complement of PR #2923's strict inclusion at non-deg. | `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace_complement_finrank_eq_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardA_zero` | **Complement saturated joint Casimir finrank**: at `\|A\| = 0`, `finrank (jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace (fun x => ! | `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace_eq_totalSpinSSquaredEigenspace_of_cardNotA_zero` | **Joint Casimir = (Ŝ_tot)² eigenspace at saturated `\|¬A\| = 0`**: `jointSublatticeCasimirEigenspace A N = Module.End.eigenspace (totalSpinSSquared Λ N).mulVecLin (saturatedFerromagnetCasimirEigenvalueS Λ N)`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_imbalance_norm_quad` | **Complement-orientation predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue** at `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`: `((s_B − s_A)·((s_B − s_A) + 1)).re = ‖biw‖·(‖biw‖ + 1)`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_imbalance_re_quad` | **Unified signed-form predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue** (no orientation hypothesis): `((s_A − s_B)·((s_A − s_B) + 1)).re = biw.re · (biw.re + 1)`. Generalises PRs #2930/#2931 via `biw.re = ±‖biw‖` (PR #2932, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedTotalSpinSquaredEigenvalueViaImbalanceRe.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_vs_predicted_gap_eq_zero_iff_saturated` | **Néel-vs-predicted (Ŝ_tot)² gap = 0 ↔ saturated edge** at `N ≥ 1`: `gap = 0 ↔ \|A\| = 0 ∨ \|¬A\| = 0`. Direct from PR #2901 (gap = min·N) + `min·N = 0 ↔ min = 0` at `N ≥ 1`. Characterises exactly which configurations have Néel matching the predicted GS Casimir eigenvalue (PR #2933, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelTotalSpinSSqGapEqZeroIffSaturated.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_sub_predictedSymm_re_eq_zero_iff_saturated` | **Energy gap iff characterisation** at `N ≥ 1`: `(<Φ_Néel\|Ĥ_toy_S\|Φ_Néel> − bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N).re = 0 ↔ \|A\| = 0 ∨ \|¬A\| = 0`. Energy parallel of PR #2933; both gaps vanish simultaneously at saturated edges (PR #2934, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelToyEnergyGapEqZeroIffSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_nonneg_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Non-negativity of predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue** at `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`: `((s_A − s_B)·((s_A − s_B) + 1)).re ≥ 0`. Direct from PR #2930 (= ‖biw‖·(‖biw‖+1)) + ‖biw‖ ≥ 0. The predicted Casimir eigenvalue is physical (≥ 0) under the proper orientation (PR #2935, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedTotalSpinSquaredEigenvalueReNonneg.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_zero_iff_balanced_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue = 0 ↔ balanced** at `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`, `N ≥ 1`: eigenvalue = 0 ↔ `\|A\| = \|¬A\|`. Direct from PR #2930 + PR #2855 (`‖biw‖ = 0 ↔ balanced`) (PR #2936, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedTotalSpinSquaredEigenvalueEqZeroIffBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_pos_of_strict_unbalanced` | **Strict positivity of predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue** at strict unbalanced (`\|¬A\| < \|A\|`, `N ≥ 1`): eigenvalue > 0. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_mMax_quad_of_cardNotA_zero` | **Saturated-edge predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue = `m_max·(m_max + 1)`**: at `\|¬A\| = 0`, eigenvalue = `(\|Λ\|·N/2)·(\|Λ\|·N/2 + 1)`. Direct from PR #2930 + PR #2851 (`‖biw‖ = m_max` at saturated). The predicted GS at saturated has the fully-polarised ferromagnetic Casimir value (PR #2938, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueSaturatedEqMMaxQuad.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_zero_of_balanced` | **Balanced-case predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue = 0 (singlet)**: at `\|A\| = \|¬A\|`, the predicted eigenvalue vanishes. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_mMax_quad_of_cardA_zero` | **Complement saturated-edge predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue = `m_max·(m_max+1)`**: at `\|A\| = 0`, complement-orientation eigenvalue = `(\|Λ\|·N/2)·(\|Λ\|·N/2 + 1)`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_mMax_quad_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Upper bound on predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue ≤ `m_max·(m_max+1)`** at `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`: predicted eigenvalue ≤ `(\|Λ\|·N/2)·(\|Λ\|·N/2 + 1)`. Direct from PR #2930 (= ‖biw‖·(‖biw‖+1)) + PR #2874 (‖biw‖ ≤ m_max) + monotonicity of `x·(x+1)`. The predicted Casimir cannot exceed the fully-polarised ferromagnetic value; equality is attained at saturated edges (PR #2942, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueLeMMaxQuad.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_mMax_quad_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Upper bound on complement predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue ≤ `m_max·(m_max+1)`** at `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`: complement-orientation eigenvalue ≤ `(\|Λ\|·N/2)·(\|Λ\|·N/2 + 1)`. Mirror of PR #2942 via PR #2931 (= ‖biw‖·(‖biw‖+1)) + PR #2874 (‖biw‖ ≤ m_max) + monotonicity of `x·(x+1)`. The complement predicted Casimir cannot exceed the fully-polarised ferromagnetic value; equality is attained at saturated complement (PR #2943, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueComplementLeMMaxQuad.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_mMax_quad` | **Unified upper bound on predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue ≤ `m_max·(m_max+1)`** with **no orientation hypothesis**: predicted eigenvalue ≤ `(\|Λ\|·N/2)·(\|Λ\|·N/2 + 1)`. Direct via PR #2932 (signed form: eigenvalue = `biw.re·(biw.re+1)`) + PR #2874 + `biw.im = 0` giving `\|biw.re\| ≤ m_max`, then `nlinarith` on the parabola `x(x+1)` on `[−m_max, m_max]`. Unifies PR #2942 (`\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`) and PR #2943 (`\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|` complement) into one orientation-free statement (PR #2944, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueLeMMaxQuadUnified.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_lt_mMax_quad_of_nondegenerate` | **Strict upper bound on predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue < `m_max·(m_max+1)` at non-degenerate** (`\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`): strengthens PR #2944 from `≤` to `<`. Direct via PR #2932 (signed form) + PR #2877 (strict imbalance-norm `‖biw‖ < m_max`) + `biw.im = 0` giving `\|biw.re\| < m_max`, then `nlinarith` on the parabola over `(−m_max, m_max)`. At non-degenerate, the predicted GS Casimir is strictly below the fully-polarised ferromagnetic value — completes the "saturated iff equality" characterisation (PR #2945, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueLtMMaxQuadNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_lt_mMax_quad_of_nondegenerate` | **Strict upper bound on complement predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue < `m_max·(m_max+1)` at non-degenerate** (`\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`, `\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`): strengthens PR #2943 from `≤` to `<`. Mirror of PR #2945. Direct via PR #2931 (complement = ‖biw‖·(‖biw‖+1)) + PR #2877 (strict imbalance-norm `‖biw‖ < m_max`) + monotonicity of `x·(x+1)` on `[0, m_max)` via `nlinarith` (PR #2946, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueComplementLtMMaxQuadNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_lt_mMax_quad_of_cardNotA_pos` | **Generalised strict predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue < `m_max·(m_max+1)` at `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`** (no `\|A\| ≥ 1`): generalises PR #2945 by removing the `\|A\| ≥ 1` hypothesis. Direct from PR #2932 (signed form) + `biw.re < m_max` strict (since `\|A\| - \|¬A\| < \|Λ\|` when `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, scaled by `N ≥ 1`) + unconditional `−m_max ≤ biw.re` (from `‖biw‖ ≤ m_max` + `biw.im = 0`), then `nlinarith` on the parabola. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_mMax_quad_iff_cardNotA_zero` | **Iff characterisation: predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue = `m_max·(m_max+1)` ↔ `\|¬A\| = 0`** (at `N ≥ 1`): the predicted Casimir hits the fully-polarised ferromagnetic value **iff** all sites are in `A` (saturated edge). Forward direction = contrapositive of PR #2947; backward = PR #2938. Completes the predicted-eigenvalue equality characterisation for the `(s_A − s_B)` orientation (PR #2948, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueEqMMaxQuadIffCardNotAZero.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_mem_Icc_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Range characterisation (complement): complement predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue ∈ `[0, m_max·(m_max+1)]`** at complement orientation `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`: mirror of PR #2950. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_eq_imbalance_re_quad_signed` | **Unified signed-form complement predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue** without orientation hypothesis: `((s_B − s_A)·((s_B − s_A) + 1)).re = biw.re·(biw.re − 1)`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_ge_neg_one_fourth` | **Lower bound: `min(canonical, complement) ≥ −1/4`** unconditionally: the parabola `x·(x−1)` has minimum `−1/4` at `x = 1/2`. Direct from PR #2967 (min = `‖biw‖·(‖biw‖−1)`) + `(x − 1/2)² ≥ 0` (PR #2973, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinGeNegOneFourth.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_zero_iff_balanced` | **Unified max iff: `max(canonical, complement) = 0 ↔ balanced`** at `N ≥ 1`: the physical predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue vanishes (singlet `S_tot = 0`) exactly at balanced sublattices. Complements PR #2971 (`max > 0 ↔ unbalanced`). Unifies PR #2936 / PR #2953 (orientation-specialised balanced iffs) via the physical max quantity (PR #2974, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqZeroIffBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_lt_mMax_quad_of_non_saturated` | **Unified strict max bound: `max(canonical, complement) < m_max·(m_max+1)`** at non-saturated (`\|A\| ≥ 1`, `\|¬A\| ≥ 1`, `N ≥ 1`): contrapositive of PR #2970 combined with PR #2972 upper bound. The physical predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalue is strictly below the fully-polarised ferromagnetic value at non-saturated. Unifies PR #2945 / PR #2946 (orientation-specialised strict bounds) via the physical max quantity (PR #2975, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxLtMMaxQuad.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_add_min_eq_two_norm_sq` | **`max + min` of canonical/complement predicted (Ŝ_tot)² eigenvalues = `2·‖biw‖²`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2968 (max − min = `2·‖biw‖`). Direct from PR #2966 + PR #2967 via `ring`. Equivalent to PR #2963 (canonical + complement sum) since `max + min = canonical + complement` (PR #2976, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxAddMin.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_sub_min_pos_iff_unbalanced` | **Iff: `max − min > 0 ↔ unbalanced`** at `N ≥ 1`: the canonical-complement eigenvalue gap (proportional to `‖biw‖`) vanishes iff balanced. Direct from PR #2968 (gap = `2·‖biw‖`) + PR #2855 (`‖biw‖ = 0 ↔ balanced`). Companion of PR #2971 (`max > 0 ↔ unbalanced`) (PR #2977, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxSubMinPosIffUnbalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_abs_canonical_sub_complement_eq_two_norm` | **`\|canonical − complement\| = 2·‖biw‖`** unconditionally: unsigned version of PR #2960. Direct from `\|2·biw.re\| = 2·\|biw.re\| = 2·‖biw‖` (using `biw.im = 0`). Equivalent to PR #2968 (`max − min = 2·‖biw‖`) since `max − min = \|canonical − complement\|` (PR #2978, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueAbsDiff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_norm_sq_add` | **`max(canonical, complement) = ‖biw‖² + ‖biw‖`** unconditionally: expanded polynomial form of PR #2966. Convenient for `nlinarith` and arithmetic manipulations on the physical max eigenvalue (PR #2979, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqNormSqAdd.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_norm_sq_sub` | **`min(canonical, complement) = ‖biw‖² − ‖biw‖`** unconditionally: expanded polynomial form of PR #2967. Companion of PR #2979 — together makes the affine parameterisation `max = ‖biw‖² + ‖biw‖`, `min = ‖biw‖² − ‖biw‖` explicit (PR #2980, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinEqNormSqSub.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_canonical_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`max(canonical, complement) = canonical`** at proper orientation `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`: the canonical orientation gives the physical predicted eigenvalue exactly at proper orientation. Direct from PR #2964 + PR #2965 + `biw.re ≥ 0` at proper orientation (PR #2981, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqCanonical.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_lt_mMax_quad_iff_non_saturated` | **Iff: `max(canonical, complement) < m_max·(m_max+1) ↔ non-saturated`** at `N ≥ 1`: the iff form of PR #2975. Direct combine of PR #2970 (`max = m_max(m_max+1) ↔ saturated`) + PR #2972 (`max ≤ m_max(m_max+1)`) (PR #2983, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxLtMMaxQuadIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_complement_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`min(canonical, complement) = complement`** at proper orientation `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`: complement is the "wrong" (min) eigenvalue at proper orientation. Companion of PR #2981 (`max = canonical` at proper orientation). Direct from PR #2964 + PR #2965 + `biw.re ≥ 0` at proper orientation (PR #2984, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinEqComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_complement_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`max(canonical, complement) = complement`** at complement orientation `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`: complement is the physical (max) eigenvalue at complement orientation. Mirror of PR #2981. Direct from PR #2964 + PR #2965 + `biw.re ≤ 0` at complement orientation (PR #2985, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_canonical_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`min(canonical, complement) = canonical`** at complement orientation `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`: canonical is the "wrong" (min) eigenvalue at complement orientation. Companion of PR #2985. Direct from PR #2964 + PR #2965 + `biw.re ≤ 0` at complement orientation (PR #2986, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinEqCanonical.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_max` | **`min(canonical, complement) ≤ max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `min_le_max` for the canonical/complement eigenvalue pair. Convenient for combining with other inequalities (PR #2987, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinLeMax.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_pos_iff_card_ne` | **Iff: `‖biw‖ > 0 ↔ unbalanced`** at `N ≥ 1`: iff form combining `‖biw‖ ≥ 0` and PR #2855 (`‖biw‖ = 0 ↔ balanced`). Convenient for switching between strict positivity and balanced/unbalanced predicates (PR #2988, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormPosIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_nonpos_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`biw.re ≤ 0` at complement orientation `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`**: mirror of the existing `_re_nonneg_of_cardA_ge_cardNotA`. Direct from `biw.re_eq` + orientation + `N ≥ 0`. Used inline by PRs #2985/#2986; consolidated as standalone lemma (PR #2989, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightReNonpos.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_canonical_le_max` | **`canonical ≤ max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `le_max_left`. Useful for chaining inequalities involving the canonical form with the physical (max) eigenvalue (PR #2990, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueCanonicalLeMax.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_complement_le_max` | **`complement ≤ max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2990. Trivial corollary of `le_max_right` (PR #2991, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueComplementLeMax.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_canonical` | **`min(canonical, complement) ≤ canonical`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `min_le_left` (PR #2992, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinLeCanonical.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_complement` | **`min(canonical, complement) ≤ complement`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2992. Trivial corollary of `min_le_right` (PR #2993, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinLeComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_canonical_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir` | **Predicted min energy decomposition**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N).re = canonical_eigenvalue.re − Re(s_A·(s_A+1)) − Re(s_B·(s_B+1))`. Direct from the definition of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` via `Complex.sub_re`. Makes explicit the relationship between predicted min energy of `H_toy` and the canonical predicted `(Ŝ_tot)²` eigenvalue (PR #2994, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReViaEigenvalue.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_max_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Predicted min energy via max (physical) eigenvalue at proper orientation**: at `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`, `(predicted_min_energy).re = max(canonical, complement) − Re(s_A·(s_A+1)) − Re(s_B·(s_B+1))`. Combines PR #2994 + PR #2981 — uses the physical predicted `(Ŝ_tot)²` eigenvalue (PR #2995, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReAtProperOrientation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_min_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Predicted min energy via min (wrong-orientation) eigenvalue at complement orientation**: at `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`, `(predicted_min_energy).re = min(canonical, complement) − Re(s_A·(s_A+1)) − Re(s_B·(s_B+1))`. Companion of PR #2995 — at complement orientation, the canonical-formula uses the smaller `min` eigenvalue, underestimating the physical min energy. Combines PR #2994 + PR #2986 (PR #2996, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReAtComplementOrientation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_add_complement` | **Complement-orientation sum identity**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N + bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (¬A) N = −\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N² − \|Λ\|·N`. Companion of the existing `_sub_complement` (gap identity). Together they give the affine sandwich: `predicted_min A = −\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2 − \|¬A\|·N` and `predicted_min (¬A) = −\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2 − \|A\|·N` (PR #2997, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAddComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_complement_iff_card_eq` | **Iff: `predicted_min_energy A = predicted_min_energy (¬A) ↔ balanced`** at `N ≥ 1`: the two orientations give the same predicted min energy **iff** balanced sublattices. Direct from the existing `_sub_complement` gap identity `= (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N` + `N ≥ 1` cancellation (PR #2998, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedEqComplementIffBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`(predicted_min A).re ≤ (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: canonical-orientation predicted min energy is bounded above by the symmetric (min-aware) form. Difference: `(predictedSymm − predicted_min A).re = (\|¬A\| − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|))·N ≥ 0`. Equality at `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|` (where `min = \|¬A\|`); strict at `\|A\| < \|¬A\|` and `N ≥ 1` (PR #2999, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReLeSymmRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`(predicted_min ¬A).re ≤ (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: mirror of PR #2999 — complement-orientation predicted min energy bounded above by symmetric form. Difference: `(predictedSymm − predicted_min ¬A).re = (\|A\| − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|))·N ≥ 0`. Equality at `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`; strict at `\|¬A\| < \|A\|` and `N ≥ 1`. Together with PR #2999 confirms `predictedSymm` as upper bound on both orientations (PR #3000, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReLeSymmRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re` | **`max((predicted_min A).re, (predicted_min ¬A).re) = (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: identifies `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm` as the **max** of orientation-specific predicted min energies. Direct from PR #2999/#3000 (upper bounds) plus equality at the orientation matching `min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)`. The symmetric form is the physical Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction (PR #3001, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxEqPredictedSymm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_of_balanced` | **At balanced: `(predicted_min A).re = (predictedSymm A).re`**. At `\|A\| = \|¬A\|`, the canonical orientation's predicted min energy equals the symmetric form (since `min = \|A\| = \|¬A\|`). Direct from the simplified forms + balanced cardinality (PR #3003, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReEqSymmAtBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_of_balanced` | **At balanced: `(predicted_min ¬A).re = (predictedSymm A).re`**. Mirror of PR #3003. Combined with PR #3003: at balanced, both orientations coincide and equal the symmetric form (PR #3004, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReEqSymmAtBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_of_cardNotA_zero` | **At canonical-saturated `\|¬A\| = 0`: `(predicted_min A).re = (predictedSymm A).re = 0`**. Both formulas vanish at the fully-polarised saturated edge (PR #3005, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReEqSymmAtSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_of_cardA_zero` | **At complement-saturated `\|A\| = 0`: `(predicted_min ¬A).re = (predictedSymm A).re = 0`**. Mirror of PR #3005. The complement-orientation predicted min energy vanishes at complement-saturated (PR #3006, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReEqSymmAtCardAZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_lt_predictedSymm_re_iff_cardA_lt_cardNotA` | **Iff strict: `(predicted_min A).re < (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: companion strict version of PR #2999 (`≤`). Direct via case-split on `\|A\| < \|¬A\|` vs `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|` and the difference computation `(\|¬A\| − min)·N` (PR #3007, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedLtSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_lt_predictedSymm_re_iff_cardNotA_lt_cardA` | **Iff strict (complement): `(predicted_min ¬A).re < (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: mirror of PR #3007. Companion strict version of PR #3000 (PR #3008, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementLtSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Iff equality: `(predicted_min A).re = (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: trichotomy companion of PR #3007 — canonical predicted min energy matches the symmetric form exactly at proper orientation. Direct from PR #2999 (`≤`) + PR #3007 (`<` iff) (PR #3009, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedEqSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Iff equality (complement): `(predicted_min ¬A).re = (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: mirror of PR #3009 — complement-orientation predicted min energy matches the symmetric form exactly at complement orientation. Direct from PR #3000 (`≤`) + PR #3008 (`<` iff) (PR #3010, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementEqSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq` | **Min of orientation-specific predicted min energies = `−\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2 − max(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`** unconditionally: companion of PR #3001 (max = predictedSymm). The min is the "unphysical" lower predicted min energy, strictly below predictedSymm at unbalanced (PR #3011, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinComplementRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_eq` | **Max − min spread of orientation-specific predicted min energies = `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N`** unconditionally: companion of PR #3001 (max = predictedSymm) and PR #3011 (min). Measures the orientation-spread of the predicted min energy — the "wrong-orientation" prediction sits exactly `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N` below the symmetric form (PR #3012, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxSubMinComplementRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_eq_zero_iff_balanced` | **Spread iff balanced: `spread = 0 ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: direct from PR #3012's spread formula `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N`. Both orientations agree exactly at balanced; at unbalanced the two orientations differ (PR #3013, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadZeroIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_pos_iff_unbalanced` | **Spread strict iff unbalanced: `0 < spread ↔ \|A\| ≠ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: companion strict version of PR #3013. Direct via `abs_pos` + `mul_pos` on PR #3012's spread formula. Completes the sign trichotomy of orientation-spread (PR #3014, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadPosIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_min_complement_re_eq` | **`(predictedSymm A).re − min(...) = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N`** unconditionally: physical interpretation — the symmetric Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction sits exactly `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N` above the "wrong-orientation" prediction. Direct from PR #3001 (max = predictedSymm) + PR #3012 (max − min spread) (PR #3015, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubMinEqAbsSpread.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff_balanced` | **`min(...) = (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: at balanced, both orientation-specific predicted min energies coincide with `predictedSymm`; at unbalanced (and `N ≥ 1`), the min sits strictly below. Direct from PR #3015 + PR #3013 (PR #3016, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_lt_predictedSymm_re_iff_unbalanced` | **`min(...) < (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| ≠ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`: companion strict version of PR #3016. Direct from PR #3015 + PR #3014. Characterises the orientation-spread of the predicted min energy relative to the symmetric prediction (PR #3017, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLtSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **Unconditional `min(...) ≤ (predictedSymm A).re`**: weak version of PR #3016/#3017 without cardinality/`N` hypothesis. Direct from PR #3001 (max = predictedSymm) + `min ≤ max` (PR #3018, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLeSymm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_predicted_re_eq` | **Explicit difference formula: `(predictedSymm A).re − (predicted_min A).re = (\|¬A\| − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|))·N`** unconditionally. At proper orientation (`\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`) the difference vanishes; at complement orientation (`\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`) the difference is `(\|¬A\| − \|A\|)·N ≥ 0` (PR #3019, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubPredictedReEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_complement_predicted_re_eq` | **Explicit difference formula (complement): `(predictedSymm A).re − (predicted_min ¬A).re = (\|A\| − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|))·N`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3019. At complement orientation (`\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`) the difference vanishes; at proper orientation the difference is `(\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N ≥ 0` (PR #3020, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubComplementPredictedReEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_complement_re_eq` | **Signed difference: `(predicted_min A).re − (predicted_min ¬A).re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N`** unconditionally. The quadratic terms `−\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2` cancel, leaving only the linear orientation gap. Positive at proper orientation, zero at balanced, negative at complement orientation (at `N ≥ 1`) (PR #3021, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReSubComplementReEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_abs_sub_complement_re_eq` | **Absolute signed difference equals spread: `\|(predicted_min A).re − (predicted_min ¬A).re\| = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N`** unconditionally. Direct from PR #3021 by `abs_mul` and `abs_of_nonneg` on `(N : ℝ)`. Matches PR #3012's spread formula `max − min = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N` — gives alternative spread characterisation via absolute signed difference (PR #3022, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAbsSubComplementEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_eq_zero_of_balanced` | **Spread vanishes at balanced (unconditional in `N`)**: `\|A\| = \|¬A\| → spread = 0`. Companion of PR #3013 covering the `N = 0` edge case where PR #3013's iff (which requires `N ≥ 1`) does not apply. Direct from PR #3012 spread formula (PR #3024, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadAtBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_complement_re_eq_zero_of_balanced` | **Signed difference vanishes at balanced (unconditional in `N`)**: `\|A\| = \|¬A\| → (pmA).re − (pm¬A).re = 0`. Companion of PR #3024 for the signed-difference form. Direct from PR #3021 (PR #3025, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSignedDiffAtBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_at_cardNotA_zero` | **Spread at canonical-saturated `\|¬A\| = 0`: `spread = \|A\|·N`**. Direct from PR #3012's spread formula. Since `\|A\| = \|Λ\|` at this edge, gives `spread = \|Λ\|·N` — the maximum possible spread (PR #3026, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadAtSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_at_cardA_zero` | **Spread at complement-saturated `\|A\| = 0`: `spread = \|¬A\|·N`**. Mirror of PR #3026 (via `zero_sub`, `abs_neg`). Since `\|¬A\| = \|Λ\|` at this edge, gives `spread = \|Λ\|·N` — the maximum spread (PR #3027, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadAtComplementSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_le_card_mul_N` | **Spread upper bound: `spread ≤ \|Λ\|·N`** unconditionally. From `\|A\| + \|¬A\| = \|Λ\|` and `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\| ≤ \|A\| + \|¬A\|`. Equality attained at saturated edges (PRs #3026/#3027) — gives the maximum possible spread (PR #3028, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadLeCardMulN.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_le_zero` | **Min of orientation-specific predicted min energies is non-positive: `min(...) ≤ 0`** unconditionally. Direct from PR #2791 (`(pmA).re ≤ 0`) + `min_le_of_left_le` (PR #3029, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinComplementReLeZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_complement_re_le_zero` | **Max of orientation-specific predicted min energies is non-positive: `max(...) ≤ 0`** unconditionally. Direct from PR #2791 applied to both orientations via `max_le`. Equivalent to PR #2844 (`(predictedSymm).re ≤ 0`) via PR #3001 (PR #3030, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxComplementReLeZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_add_min_complement_re_eq` | **`max + min` of orientation-specific predicted min energies = `−\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N² − \|Λ\|·N`** unconditionally. Direct from `max + min = sum` (general identity) + the complex-level sum identity `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_add_complement`. Companion of PR #3012 (max − min spread) and PR #3001/PR #3011 (PR #3031, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxAddMinComplementRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_sub_spread` | **`min(...) = (predictedSymm A).re − \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N`** unconditionally. Rearrangement of PR #3015. Third explicit form of `min` (alongside PR #3001/#3011 / PR #3011 direct form) (PR #3032, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqSymmSubSpread.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_eq` | **Arithmetic mean of orientation-specific predicted min energies: `((pmA).re + (pm¬A).re) / 2 = −\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2 − \|Λ\|·N/2`** unconditionally. The midpoint of the two orientations — equals `predictedSymm − \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N/2` (half the spread below the symmetric prediction). Direct from PR #3031 (PR #3033, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgComplementRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_avg_complement_re_eq` | **Half-spread separation: `(predictedSymm A).re − avg = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N / 2`** unconditionally. The symmetric Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction sits exactly half the orientation-spread above the arithmetic mean of the two orientations. Direct from PR #3001 (max = predictedSymm) + PR #3012 (spread) + `max_add_min` (PR #3034, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubAvgEqHalfSpread.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`avg ≤ (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally. Weak version of PR #3034 (equality with gap `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N/2`). Direct from PR #3001 + `le_max_left/right` (PR #3035, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgLeSymm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_le_avg` | **`min(...) ≤ avg`** unconditionally. Lower companion of PR #3035: the orientation-specific min sits below the arithmetic mean. Direct from `min_le_left/right` + linarith (PR #3036, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLeAvg.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff_balanced` | **`avg = (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. At balanced both orientation-specific predicted min energies coincide with `predictedSymm`, so their average also equals `predictedSymm`. Direct from PR #3034 (half-spread separation) (PR #3037, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgEqSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_lt_predictedSymm_re_iff_unbalanced` | **`avg < (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| ≠ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Companion strict version of PR #3037 — completes the sign trichotomy of the `avg`–`predictedSymm` gap. Direct from PR #3034 + abs_pos (PR #3038, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgLtSymmIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_sub_min_eq` | **`avg − min = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N / 2`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3034. Together they give the symmetric sandwich `predictedSymm − avg = avg − min = \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N/2` — the average is exactly half-spread below max (= predictedSymm) and half-spread above min (PR #3039, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgSubMinEqHalfSpread.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_avg_iff_balanced` | **`min(...) = avg ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3037 for the lower end of the orientation sandwich. At balanced min = max = avg; at unbalanced min sits strictly below avg by half-spread (PR #3040, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqAvgIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_lt_avg_iff_unbalanced` | **`min(...) < avg ↔ \|A\| ≠ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3038. Companion strict version of PR #3040. Direct from PR #3039 + abs_pos (PR #3041, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLtAvgIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_le_zero` | **`avg ≤ 0`** unconditional. Direct from PR #2791 applied to both orientations (both non-positive, so average is) (PR #3042, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgLeZero.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_eq_avg_add_half_spread` | **`(predictedSymm A).re = avg + \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N/2`** unconditionally. Rearrangement of PR #3034 — `predictedSymm` is the midpoint plus the half-spread (PR #3043, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmEqAvgAddHalfSpread.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_avg_sub_half_spread` | **`min(...) = avg − \|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N/2`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3043 for the lower end of the orientation sandwich (PR #3045, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqAvgSubHalfSpread.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_complement_at_proper` | **min identification at proper orientation: `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\| → min(...) = (predicted_min ¬A).re`**. The min is realised by the complement orientation at proper. Direct from PR #3021 + `min_eq_right` (PR #3046, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqComplementAtProper.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_canonical_at_complement` | **min identification at complement orientation: `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\| → min(...) = (predicted_min A).re`**. Mirror of PR #3046 for complement orientation (PR #3047, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqCanonicalAtComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_complement_re_eq_canonical_at_proper` | **max identification at proper orientation: `\|¬A\| ≤ \|A\| → max(...) = (predicted_min A).re`**. Mirror of PR #3046 for the max side — consistent with PR #3009 via PR #3001 (PR #3048, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxEqCanonicalAtProper.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_complement_re_eq_complement_at_complement` | **max identification at complement orientation: `\|A\| ≤ \|¬A\| → max(...) = (predicted_min ¬A).re`**. Mirror of PR #3048 for complement orientation — consistent with PR #3010 via PR #3001 (PR #3049, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxEqComplementAtComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_neg_of_nondegenerate` | **`avg < 0` at non-degenerate (`\|A\| ≥ 1, \|¬A\| ≥ 1, N ≥ 1`)**. Strict version of PR #3042: both orientations strictly negative (PR #2817), so average is too (PR #3050, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgReNegNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_avg_complement_re_eq` | **Operator-algebraic bridge: `⟨Φ_Néel \| Ĥ_toy \| Φ_Néel⟩.re − avg = \|Λ\|·N / 2`** unconditionally. The Néel-state toy-Hamiltonian expectation sits exactly `\|Λ\|·N/2` above the orientation-pair average — bridges the existing Néel-state expectation framework (PR #1178/#1193) with the predicted min energy avg (PR #3033) (PR #3051, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubAvg.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_min_complement_re_eq` | **Operator-algebraic bridge: `⟨Φ_Néel \| Ĥ_toy \| Φ_Néel⟩.re − min(...) = max(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`** unconditionally. The Néel-state toy-Hamiltonian expectation sits exactly `max(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N` above the orientation-pair min — completing the affine triangle of bridges (PR #3052, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubMinComplement.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_canonical_predicted_re_eq` | **Operator-algebraic bridge: `⟨Φ_Néel \| Ĥ_toy \| Φ_Néel⟩.re − (predicted_min A).re = \|¬A\|·N`** unconditionally. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_complement_predicted_re_eq` | **Operator-algebraic bridge (complement): `⟨Φ_Néel \| Ĥ_toy \| Φ_Néel⟩.re − (predicted_min ¬A).re = \|A\|·N`** unconditionally. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_gt_canonical_predicted_re` | **Strict version of PR #3053**: `0 < \|¬A\|, 0 < N → (pmA).re < ⟨Néel⟩.re`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_gt_complement_predicted_re` | **Strict version of PR #3054 (mirror)**: `0 < \|A\|, 0 < N → (pm¬A).re < ⟨Néel⟩.re` (PR #3057, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationGtComplementPredictedNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_gt_avg_complement_re` | **Strict version of PR #3051**: `0 < \|Λ\|, 0 < N → avg < ⟨Néel⟩.re`. Néel state strictly above the orientation-pair midpoint at any non-trivial lattice + positive spin (PR #3058, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationGtAvgNondegenerate.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_gt_min_complement_re` | **Strict version of PR #3052**: `0 < \|Λ\|, 0 < N → min(...) < ⟨Néel⟩.re`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_eq_canonical_predicted_re_iff_cardNotA_zero` | **Equality version of PR #3056**: at `N ≥ 1`, `⟨Néel⟩.re = (pmA).re ↔ \|¬A\| = 0`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_eq_complement_predicted_re_iff_cardA_zero` | **Equality version of PR #3057 (mirror)**: at `N ≥ 1`, `⟨Néel⟩.re = (pm¬A).re ↔ \|A\| = 0`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_eq_avg_complement_re_iff_card_zero` | **Equality version of PR #3058**: at `N ≥ 1`, `⟨Néel⟩.re = avg ↔ \|Λ\| = 0`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_eq_min_complement_re_iff_card_zero` | **Equality version of PR #3059**: at `N ≥ 1`, `⟨Néel⟩.re = min(...) ↔ \|Λ\| = 0`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff_saturated` | **Direct equality form: at `N ≥ 1`, `⟨Néel⟩.re = (predictedSymm A).re ↔ \|A\| = 0 ∨ \|¬A\| = 0`**. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_complement_re_iff_balanced` | **Real-part orientation iff: at `N ≥ 1`, `(pmA).re = (pm¬A).re ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`**. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_lt_complement_re_iff_cardA_lt_cardNotA` | **Strict ordering iff: at `N ≥ 1`, `(pmA).re < (pm¬A).re ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\|`**. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_lt_re_iff_cardNotA_lt_cardA` | **Strict ordering iff (mirror): at `N ≥ 1`, `(pm¬A).re < (pmA).re ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\|`**. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_le_complement_re_iff_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Non-strict ordering iff: at `N ≥ 1`, `(pmA).re ≤ (pm¬A).re ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`**. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_le_re_iff_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Non-strict ordering iff (mirror): at `N ≥ 1`, `(pm¬A).re ≤ (pmA).re ↔ \|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`**. Mirror of PR #3069 via swap argument (PR #3070, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReLeReIff.lean`) |

| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_eq_complement_orientation` | **Sublattice-swap symmetry of Néel-state toy-Hamiltonian expectation**: `⟨Φ_Néel(A)\|Ĥ_toy\|Φ_Néel(A)⟩ = ⟨Φ_Néel(¬A)\|Ĥ_toy\|Φ_Néel(¬A)⟩` unconditionally. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_complement_re_sublattice_swap` | **Sublattice-swap symmetry of orientation-pair average**: `avg(A) = avg(¬A)` unconditionally. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_sum_complement_re_sublattice_swap` | **Sublattice-swap symmetry of orientation-pair sum** unconditionally (PR #3118, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSumSubLatticeSwap.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_complement_re_sublattice_swap` | **Sublattice-swap symmetry of orientation-pair max** unconditionally — `max_comm` after `¬¬A = A` (PR #3119, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxSubLatticeSwap.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_sublattice_swap` | **Sublattice-swap symmetry of orientation-pair min** unconditionally — `min_comm` after `¬¬A = A` (PR #3120, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinSubLatticeSwap.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_sublattice_swap` | **Sublattice-swap symmetry of orientation-pair spread** unconditionally — combines PR #3119 + PR #3120 (PR #3121, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadSubLatticeSwap.lean`) |

Connects orientation-spread to the Tasaki §2.5 Theorem 2.3 imbalance-weight norm (twice the predicted total-spin norm). Direct from PR #3012 + `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq` (PR #3122, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadEqTwoBiwNorm.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_avg_sub_biw_norm` | **`min(...) = avg − ‖biw‖`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3125 — direct from PR #3045 + `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_eq` (PR #3126, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqAvgSubBiwNorm.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_add_min_complement_re_eq_two_avg` | **`max(...) + min(...) = 2 · avg`** unconditionally. Generic `max + min = sum` identity packaged for orientation-pair (PR #3127, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMaxAddMinEqTwoAvg.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_max_complement_re_eq` | **Operator-algebraic bridge: `⟨Φ_Néel \| Ĥ_toy \| Φ_Néel⟩.re − max(...) = min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N`** unconditionally. Composes PR #3001 (`max = predictedSymm.re`) with `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_sub_predictedSymm`, closing the operator–algebraic affine triangle (Néel − min = max·N; Néel − avg = \|Λ\|·N/2; Néel − max = min·N) (PR #3129, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubMaxComplement.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_min_add_sub_max_complement_re_eq` | **Additive identity for the two gaps: `(⟨Néel⟩.re − min) + (⟨Néel⟩.re − max) = \|Λ\|·N`** unconditionally. Combines PRs #3052 + #3129 with `min + max = \|A\| + \|¬A\| = \|Λ\|`. Equivalent to `2 · (⟨Néel⟩.re − avg) = \|Λ\|·N`, consistent with PR #3051's gap-to-avg formula (PR #3130, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubMinAddSubMax.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_min_mul_sub_max_complement_re_eq` | **Multiplicative identity for the two gaps: `(⟨Néel⟩.re − min) · (⟨Néel⟩.re − max) = \|A\| · \|¬A\| · N²`** unconditionally. Combines PRs #3052 + #3129 with `min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|) · max(\|A\|, \|¬A\|) = \|A\| · \|¬A\|`. Directly connects the operator-algebraic gap product to the leading quadratic term `−\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2` of the predicted minimum energy (PR #3131, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubMinMulSubMax.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_min_sub_sub_max_complement_re_eq` | **Difference identity for the two gaps: `(⟨Néel⟩.re − min) − (⟨Néel⟩.re − max) = (max(\|A\|, \|¬A\|) − min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)) · N`** unconditionally. Equals `\|\|A\| − \|¬A\|\|·N = 2·‖biw‖` (the orientation spread, twice the imbalance-weight norm). Combines PRs #3052 + #3129 with `ring` (PR #3132, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubMinSubSubMax.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_lt_max_complement_re_iff_unbalanced` | **`min((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) < max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|A\| ≠ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. The orientation min and max coincide exactly at balanced; off-balanced the spread `max − min = 2·‖biw‖` is positive. Uses `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_complement_re_iff_balanced` (PR #3066) plus `min/max` case analysis (PR #3133, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLtMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_eq_max_complement_re_iff_balanced` | **`min((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) = max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3133 — the orientation min and max coincide exactly at balanced (spread = 0). Uses PR #3066 + `min/max` case analysis (PR #3134, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinEqMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_le_avg_complement_re_iff_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`(pmA).re ≤ avg ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Uses `x ≤ (x+y)/2 ↔ x ≤ y` composed with PR #3070 (PR #3135, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReLeAvgIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_le_avg_complement_re_iff_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`(pm¬A).re ≤ avg ↔ \|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Uses `x ≤ (x+y)/2 ↔ x ≤ y` composed with PR #3070 applied to `¬A` then `¬¬A = A`-fold (PR #3136, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReLeAvgIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_lt_avg_complement_re_iff_cardA_lt_cardNotA` | **`(pmA).re < avg ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_lt_avg_complement_re_iff_cardNotA_lt_cardA` | **`(pm¬A).re < avg ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3137 — the complement predicted-min energy sits strictly below the orientation-pair average exactly when `¬A` is strictly lighter. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_avg_complement_re_iff_balanced` | **`(pmA).re = avg ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Equality version: the canonical predicted-min energy equals the orientation-pair average exactly at balanced — where `(pmA).re = (pm¬A).re = avg`. Uses `x = (x+y)/2 ↔ x = y` composed with PR #3066 (PR #3139, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReEqAvgIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_avg_complement_re_iff_balanced` | **`(pm¬A).re = avg ↔ \|¬A\| = \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3139 — the complement predicted-min energy equals the orientation-pair average exactly at balanced. Uses `y = (x+y)/2 ↔ y = x` composed with PR #3066 (PR #3140, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReEqAvgIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_lt_max_complement_re_iff_unbalanced` | **`avg < max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|A\| ≠ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Upper companion of PR #3040 (`min < avg ↔ unbalanced`). Via PR #3127 (`max + min = 2·avg`), the equivalence `avg < max ↔ min < avg` reduces to the lower-side strict iff (PR #3141, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAvgLtMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_avg_eq_max_complement_re_iff_balanced` | **`avg = max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Equality companion of PR #3141. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_lt_max_complement_re_iff_cardA_lt_cardNotA` | **`(pmA).re < max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Uses `x < max x y ↔ x < y` composed with PR #3067 (PR #3143, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReLtMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_lt_re_complement_re_iff_cardNotA_lt_cardA` | **`min((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) < (pmA).re ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. The orientation-pair min sits strictly below the canonical predicted-min energy exactly when `¬A` is strictly lighter (i.e. `min = (pm¬A).re`). Uses `min x y < x ↔ y < x` composed with PR #3067 applied to `¬A` then `¬¬A = A`-fold (PR #3144, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLtReIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_lt_complement_re_iff_cardA_lt_cardNotA` | **`min((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) < (pm¬A).re ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3144 — the orientation-pair min sits strictly below the complement predicted-min energy exactly when `A` is strictly lighter (i.e. `min = (pmA).re`). Uses `min x y < y ↔ x < y` composed with PR #3067 (PR #3145, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedMinLtComplementReIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_lt_max_complement_re_iff_cardNotA_lt_cardA` | **`(pm¬A).re < max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3143 — the complement predicted-min energy sits strictly below the orientation-pair max exactly when `¬A` is strictly lighter. Uses `y < max x y ↔ y < x` composed with PR #3067 applied to `¬A` then `¬¬A = A`-fold (PR #3146, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReLtMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_max_complement_re_iff_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`(pmA).re = max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|¬A\| ≤ \|A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. The canonical predicted-min energy equals the orientation-pair max exactly when `A` is at least as heavy as `¬A` (max equals `(pmA).re` precisely when `(pm¬A).re ≤ (pmA).re`). Uses `x = max x y ↔ y ≤ x` composed with PR #3070 applied to `¬A` then `¬¬A = A`-fold (PR #3147, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReEqMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_max_complement_re_iff_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`(pm¬A).re = max((pmA).re, (pm¬A).re) ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\|`** at `N ≥ 1`. Mirror of PR #3147 — the complement predicted-min energy equals the orientation-pair max exactly when `¬A` is at least as heavy as `A`. Uses `y = max x y ↔ x ≤ y` composed with PR #3070 (PR #3148, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReEqMaxIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_neg_iff_complement_nondeg` | **Strict negativity iff for the canonical predicted-min energy**: `(pmA).re < 0 ↔ \|¬A\| > 0 ∧ N > 0`. Uses the simplified form `(pmA).re = −\|A\|·\|¬A\|·N²/2 − \|¬A\|·N` and shows the bracket factor `\|A\|·N²/2 + N` is positive iff `N > 0`. Canonical companion to existing complement / symm / avg / max / min strict-negativity iffs (PR #3150, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReNegIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_pos_iff` | **Strict positivity iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `0 < biw.re ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\| ∧ 0 < N`. Uses `biw.re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N/2` and case analysis on the sign of the cardinality difference. Strict companion to existing `re_nonneg_of_cardA_ge_cardNotA` (PR #3151, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightRePosIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_neg_iff` | **Strict negativity iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `biw.re < 0 ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\| ∧ 0 < N`. Mirror of PR #3151 — uses `biw.re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N/2` and case analysis on the sign of the cardinality difference and `N`. Strict companion to existing `re_nonpos_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` (PR #3152, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightReNegIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_eq_zero_iff` | **Vanishing iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `biw.re = 0 ↔ \|A\| = \|¬A\| ∨ N = 0`. Direct from `mul_eq_zero` on `biw.re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N/2`. Together with PRs #3151 + #3152, classifies the sign of `biw.re` completely: positive iff `|¬A| < |A| ∧ N > 0`; negative iff `|A| < |¬A| ∧ N > 0`; zero iff balanced or `N = 0` (PR #3153, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightReEqZeroIff.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_sub_predictedSymm_re_pos_iff_nondeg` | **iff form of the Néel–predictedSymm gap positivity**: `0 < ⟨Néel⟩ − predictedSymm \.re ↔ 0 < \|A\| ∧ 0 < \|¬A\| ∧ 0 < N`. Uses the explicit gap formula `(⟨Néel⟩ − predictedSymm).re = min(\|A\|, \|¬A\|)·N` then `min·N > 0 ↔ min > 0 ∧ N > 0 ↔ \|A\|, \|¬A\|, N all > 0`. Iff strengthening of existing `..._pos_of_nondegenerate` (PR #3154, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSPredictedSymmGapPosIffNondeg.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_nonneg_iff` | **Non-strict non-negativity iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `0 ≤ biw.re ↔ \|¬A\| ≤ \|A\| ∨ N = 0`. Direct from sign argument on `biw.re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N/2`. Together with PRs #3151/#3152/#3153 completes the half of biw.re sign classification — see also PR #3156 for the non-positivity mirror (PR #3155, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightReNonnegIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_nonpos_iff` | **Non-strict non-positivity iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `biw.re ≤ 0 ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\| ∨ N = 0`. Mirror of PR #3155 — direct from sign argument on `biw.re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N/2`. Together with PRs #3151/#3152/#3153/#3155 completes the full sign classification of `biw.re` (positive / negative / zero / non-negative / non-positive iffs) (PR #3156, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightReNonposIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_avg_complement_re_eq_biw_re` | **Algebraic bridge: `(pmA).re − avg = biw.re`** unconditionally. The canonical predicted-min energy minus the orientation-pair average equals the imbalance-weight real part. From `x − (x+y)/2 = (x − y)/2` plus explicit `pmA − pm¬A = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N`. Sign-aware refinement of PR #3122 (`spread = 2·‖biw‖`) and PR #3123 (`avg = predictedSymm − ‖biw‖`) (PR #3157, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReSubAvgEqBiwRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_sub_avg_complement_re_eq_neg_biw_re` | **Algebraic bridge: `(pm¬A).re − avg = −biw.re`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3157 — the complement predicted-min energy minus the orientation-pair average equals the negative imbalance-weight real part `−biw.re = (\|¬A\| − \|A\|)·N/2` (PR #3158, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReSubAvgEqNegBiwRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_complement_re_eq_two_biw_re` | **Signed spread: `(pmA).re − (pm¬A).re = 2 · biw.re`** unconditionally. Direct corollary of PRs #3157 + #3158 via linarith. Signed companion of PR #3122 (`spread = 2·‖biw‖`); matches existing PR #3021 (`= (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N`) since `2·biw.re = (\|A\| − \|¬A\|)·N` (PR #3159, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReSubComplementReEqTwoBiwRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_sub_re_eq_neg_two_biw_re` | **Reverse signed spread: `(pm¬A).re − (pmA).re = −2 · biw.re`** unconditionally. Direct negation of PR #3159 via linarith (PR #3160, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReSubReEqNegTwoBiwRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_re_eq_biw_norm_sub_biw_re` | **Gap to symmetric max from canonical orientation: `predictedSymm.re − (pmA).re = ‖biw‖ − biw.re`** unconditionally. Composes PR #3001 (`max = predictedSymm.re`) + PR #3125 (`max = avg + ‖biw‖`) + PR #3157 (`(pmA).re − avg = biw.re`). Geometric content: zero at `\|A\| ≥ \|¬A\|` (canonical orientation already achieves symm max), `−2·biw.re > 0` at `\|A\| < \|¬A\|` (PR #3161, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmReSubReEqBiwNormSubBiwRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_complement_re_eq_biw_norm_add_biw_re` | **Gap to symmetric max from complement orientation: `predictedSymm.re − (pm¬A).re = ‖biw‖ + biw.re`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3161. Composes PR #3001 + PR #3125 + PR #3158 (`(pm¬A).re − avg = −biw.re`). Geometric content: `2·biw.re ≥ 0` at `\|A\| ≥ \|¬A\|`; zero at `\|A\| < \|¬A\|` (complement orientation already attains symm max) (PR #3162, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmReSubComplementReEqBiwNormAddBiwRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_lt_predictedSymm_re_iff` | **Strict gap to symmetric form: `(pmA).re < predictedSymm.re ↔ \|A\| < \|¬A\| ∧ 0 < N`**. The canonical predicted-min energy sits strictly below the symmetric form exactly when canonical is the heavier orientation (or trivially in case `N = 0` where they coincide). Uses PR #3161 (gap = `‖biw‖ − biw.re`) + PR #3152 (`biw.re < 0` iff). Strict refinement of existing non-strict `re_le_predictedSymm_re` (PR #3163, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReLtSymmReIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_lt_predictedSymm_re_iff` | **Strict gap to symmetric form (complement): `(pm¬A).re < predictedSymm.re ↔ \|¬A\| < \|A\| ∧ 0 < N`**. Mirror of PR #3163. Uses PR #3162 (gap = `‖biw‖ + biw.re`) + PR #3151 (`biw.re > 0` iff). Strict refinement of existing non-strict `complement_re_le_predictedSymm_re` (PR #3164, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReLtSymmReIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff` | **Equality to symmetric form: `(pmA).re = predictedSymm.re ↔ \|¬A\| ≤ \|A\| ∨ N = 0`**. Equality companion of the strict iff PR #3163. The canonical predicted-min energy coincides with the symmetric form exactly when canonical is at least as heavy as complement (so canonical attains the max) or `N = 0` (degenerate). Uses PR #3161 + PR #3155 (PR #3165, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReEqSymmReIff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff` | **Equality to symmetric form (complement): `(pm¬A).re = predictedSymm.re ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\| ∨ N = 0`**. Mirror of PR #3165. Uses PR #3162 (gap = `‖biw‖ + biw.re`) + PR #3156 (`biw.re ≤ 0 ↔ \|A\| ≤ \|¬A\| ∨ N = 0`) (PR #3166, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedComplementReEqSymmReIff.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_canonical_predicted_re_pos_iff` | **iff form of Néel–(pmA) gap positivity**: `0 < ⟨Néel⟩.re − (pmA).re ↔ 0 < \|¬A\| ∧ 0 < N`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_complement_predicted_re_pos_iff` | **iff form of Néel–(pm¬A) gap positivity**: `0 < ⟨Néel⟩.re − (pm¬A).re ↔ 0 < \|A\| ∧ 0 < N`. Mirror of PR #3167. Uses existing gap formula `⟨Néel⟩.re − (pm¬A).re = \|A\|·N` (PR #3168, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSExpectationSubComplementPosIff.lean`) |
| `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_avg_complement_re_pos_iff` | **iff form of Néel–avg gap positivity**: `0 < ⟨Néel⟩.re − avg ↔ 0 < \|Λ\| ∧ 0 < N`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_re_sub_complement_predicted_re_eq_half_card_add_biw_re` | **biw-form of complement gap: `⟨Néel⟩.re − (pm¬A).re = \|Λ\|·N/2 + biw.re`** unconditionally.
